label: 15_africa news_nigerian_sudan_nigeria
description: The narratives focused on the ongoing conflicts in Nigeria and South Sudan, particularly the Boko Haram insurgency. They also covered economic issues like South Africa's widening current account deficit and U.S. investigations into corruption within FIFA involving South African officials.

earliest_articles.title: Africa in the News: Kenyatta and the ICC, Escalating Violence in the Central African Republic
earliest_articles.excerpt: As part of our new blog, Africa in Focus, we will do a weekly news roundup every Friday for our readers on the major news stories related to Africa. The roundup will focus on the top stories of the week providing a balance of opinions, commentary and analysis. This week’s Africa news mainly focused on the Kenyan…

earliest_articles.title: Africa in the News: Madagascar Elections, 1992 Mozambique Peace Agreement Fails, Kenya’s William Ruto to Attend ICC Trial
earliest_articles.excerpt: This marks the second edition of the weekly “Africa in the News” roundup that we will be doing every Friday for our readers on the major news stories related to Africa. This week’s Africa news has focused a on the election in Madagascar, the failed peace agreement in Mozambique, and the ICC trial for William Ruto.…

earliest_articles.title: Africa in the News: Ghana and Zambia Downgraded by Fitch, IMF Forecasts Increased Vulnerability for Sub-Saharan Africa, and Multinational Oil Companies Feel the Impact of Continued Conflict
earliest_articles.excerpt: Each Friday the Africa Growth Initiative brings you a collection of news stories related to Africa. newest_articles.excerpt: Experts predict uneven economic growth across sub-Saharan Africa On Wednesday, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) announced that the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is expected to grow 5.4 percent in 2021 and 6.4 percent in 2022.
